prompt: A FlowForge SOP Canvas workspace where product managers, process owners, and frontline supervisors can draft and finalize SOPs, approval chains, and incident response paths in an internet-isolated conference room using a single, offline browser app. The primary experience is a zoomable canvas with draggable nodes, snap-to-grid alignment guides, and connector lines that default to arrowed direction; users can toggle routing between orthogonal elbows and smooth curves. The node library is intentionally constrained to five card types—Start, End, Decision, Action, and Note—each with a consistent header, optional owner/department tag, and a short description field, plus a right-side Inspector Drawer for editing details and style (color, icon, and status). A typical session starts from a template or a blank diagram, then flows are built by drag-in, auto-alignment, and quick-connect handles; the app must support up to 500 cards and 800 connections per diagram, zoom from 10% to 400%, and Undo/Redo up to 200 steps with a visible history in a Modal. To support quality, safety, and traceability, each published diagram can generate local traceability codes for selected steps (human-readable like “SOP-042-D3”), provide a verification view that highlights matching nodes, and maintain an inspection/results Table attached to the diagram (pass/fail, notes, timestamp, and reviewer name). “Publish/Unpublish” is a local state that controls whether a diagram appears in the “Approved Library,” and retractions must record a reason. Roles are UI personas only (Author, Reviewer, Viewer) that change available menus and prompts for consistency, not as a security boundary. Local sign-in is limited to a username plus a locally stored password (minimum 8 characters) and optional “real name / organization” profile fields; the app should mask sensitive fields on-screen by default (for example, showing “J*** S****” in shared rooms) and support risky-user tagging and a local blacklist label for internal handling. Built as a Vue.js SPA using a reactive store, all data stays on the device: lightweight preferences (canvas grid on/off, last zoom, theme, recent files) persist in LocalStorage, while diagrams, version snapshots, inspection records, and embedded images are stored in IndexedDB. Autosave runs every 10 seconds while the app is open, keeping the most recent 20 versions per diagram with one-click rollback; scheduled behavior is “best effort” and only executes while the app is running. Bulk import reads a user-selected JSON file via the File API and can load up to 1,000 nodes and relationships; duplicates are removed when name and card type match, and validation failures must report exact JSON path/row context and field (e.g., missing nodeId, dangling connection endpoint) in a Toast plus an Import Errors Modal. Export supports JSON, SVG, and PNG; PNG rendering must cap the longest edge at 8,000 pixels and use a Web Worker for rasterization to keep the UI responsive. Multi-tab usage is coordinated with BroadcastChannel to warn about concurrent edits and show “newer version available” prompts. A Service Worker may be used to cache static assets for installable, offline-first behavior, and all “privacy/compliance” actions (data deletion, audit retention notes) are implemented as local-only controls with explicit user confirmation and file-based backups.
